description_long: This helicopter features a whimsical, cartoon-like design with a rounded fuselage and exaggerated proportions. The nose is painted bright red with a yellow jagged pattern resembling teeth, giving it a playful, aggressive look. Two large, spherical engine pods with propellers are mounted on either side of the cockpit, and the tail is adorned with a green, finned stabilizer and a small tail rotor. The main rotor is long and thin, with four blades extending from the central hub. The landing skids are robust and angular, supporting the body above the ground. The cockpit is open, with visible seating and controls, and the overall color scheme includes shades of gray, green, and metallic accents.
description: A cartoonish helicopter with a red, toothy nose, large side engine pods, and a green finned tail. It features an open cockpit, long main rotor, and robust landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the red, jagged-nosed cockpit, large side engine pods with propellers, and the full width of the main rotor.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated body, open cockpit, green tail fin, and the angular landing skids.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the green tail fin, small tail rotor, and the symmetrical placement of the engine pods.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the cockpit opening, engine pod details, and the structure of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades made of metal, colored gray with subtle markings at the hub. The blades are thin and extend symmetrically from the central rotor hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small, two-bladed tail rotor is mounted vertically at the end of the green tail fin, colored gray with no visible markings or protective structures.
attribute_landing_skids: The landing skids are angular, robust, and metallic gray, with crossbars connecting the two skids for added stability. They are designed to keep the helicopter elevated above the ground.
attribute_cockpit_windows: No traditional cockpit windows are visible; the cockpit appears open with no glass enclosure, exposing the seating and controls.
